toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Add Arize evaluations to Twyne

## Context

Twyne's server-side AI (persona feedback, synthesis, rubric review) runs in Convex
`"use node"` actions in `convex/agents.ts`, calling the Vercel AI SDK `generateText`
(7 call sites), routed through the Bifrost gateway (`neuralwatt/*`, `anthropic/*`).
Today the only observability is a fire-and-forget PostHog hook
(`convex/posthog.ts` → `captureServerAiGeneration`, called at each site).

We want **Arize evaluations**: (A) stream real AI calls to Arize as OpenInference
traces, (B) run LLM-as-judge evaluators on those traces, and (C) an offline
dataset/experiment harness for CI regression. The Arize skills (installed under
`.agents/skills/arize-*`) drive B and C via the `ax` CLI.

**Decisions already made:** Both online + offline. Arize creds in hand and stored
in `skate` (`arize_api_key`, `arize_space_id`). The evaluator **judge** reuses the
Bifrost gateway via an Arize `custom` AI integration.

Key constraint: Convex actions are short-lived/serverless. Per Arize's Vercel docs,
use `OpenInferenceSimpleSpanProcessor` (exports each span synchronously on end —
**not** `BatchSpanProcessor`, which drops spans when the function freezes) plus an
explicit `await provider.forceFlush()` before the action returns.

---

## Phase A — Live tracing (code)

**New file `convex/arizeTracing.ts`** — singleton tracer, registered at module load:
- `NodeTracerProvider` with `resourceFromAttributes({ model_id: ARIZE_PROJECT_NAME, model_version })`.
- `OpenInferenceSimpleSpanProcessor({ exporter: new OTLPTraceExporter({ url: "https://otlp.arize.com/v1/traces", headers: { "arize-space-id": process.env.ARIZE_SPACE_ID, "arize-api-key": process.env.ARIZE_API_KEY } }), spanFilter: isOpenInferenceSpan, reparentOrphanedSpans: true })`.
- Guard: if `ARIZE_API_KEY`/`ARIZE_SPACE_ID` are unset, skip `register()` and make
  `flushArize()` a no-op — mirrors the dormant-when-unconfigured behavior of
  `posthog.ts`. Export `provider`, `tracingEnabled`, and `async flushArize()`.

**Deps to add** (`package.json`), pinned per Arize docs:
`@arizeai/openinference-vercel@^2`, `@opentelemetry/api`,
`@opentelemetry/exporter-trace-otlp-proto`, `@opentelemetry/resources`,
`@opentelemetry/sdk-trace-node`. (`ai`/`@ai-sdk/*` already present.)

**Edit `convex/agents.ts`**:
- `import "./arizeTracing"` at top (forces provider registration) + import `flushArize`.
- Add to each of the 7 `generateText({...})` calls:
  `experimental_telemetry: { isEnabled: tracingEnabled, functionId: <feature/spanName>, metadata: { feature, persona: req.persona.id, provider: provider.label, model: provider.modelId, instruction } }`.
  Reuse the same `feature`/`spanName`/persona values already passed to
  `captureServerAiGeneration` so PostHog and Arize stay consistent.
- Call `await flushArize()` in each action right before it returns its result (and in
  the catch paths), so synchronously-exported spans finish posting before freeze.

**Env** (set in **Convex deployment** env, like the Bifrost/PostHog vars — see
`twyne-prod-env-split` memory): `ARIZE_API_KEY`, `ARIZE_SPACE_ID`,
`ARIZE_PROJECT_NAME=twyne`. Document them in `.env.example` next to the Bifrost block.
Values come from `skate get arize_api_key` / `arize_space_id`; I will reference the
env vars in code and run `npx convex env set` (not hardcode the secret).

## Phase B — Evaluators (ax CLI, no app-code change)

1. Install CLI: `uv tool install arize-ax-cli` (fallback `pipx`/`pip`); needs `ax >= 0.19`.
2. Profile: configure `ax` with `arize_api_key` + `arize_space_id` (per
   `arize-instrumentation/references/ax-profiles.md`).
3. Judge AI integration reusing Bifrost:
   `ax ai-integrations create --name "bifrost-judge" --provider custom \
     --base-url "https://bifrost.dio.computer/openai/v1" \
     --auth-type proxy_with_headers --headers '{"x-bifrost-api-key":"<bifrost key>"}'`
   Judge model: `anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6` (available on the gateway), `temperature 0`.
4. Create evaluators with `arize-evaluator` skill — start with **correctness/helpfulness**
   and **faithfulness-to-draft** (the persona feedback must engage the actual draft text).
   Column-map template vars to the OpenInference span fields emitted in Phase A
   (`attributes.input.value`, `attributes.output.value`, plus our `metadata.*`).
5. Attach a **continuous task** on the `twyne` project (sampling rate < 1 to start) so
   new spans get scored automatically.

## Phase C — Offline experiments / CI harness

- New `evals/` dir: `evals/dataset.jsonl` (representative persona-feedback + rubric-review
  inputs) and `evals/run-experiment.ts` — a node script that replays each example through
  the **existing prompt builders** in `convex/agentPrompts.ts` against Bifrost (no Convex
  needed), producing outputs.
- Upload + score:
  `ax datasets create` → `ax experiments create --name <model/date> --dataset twyne-evals --file runs.json`
  → reuse the Phase B evaluators on the experiment.
- Add an `npm run eval` script; document running it in CI for regression/model-compare.

---

## Reuse / references
- Dormant-when-unconfigured pattern: `convex/posthog.ts:62` (`captureEnabled`).
- Feature/persona metadata already assembled at each call: `convex/agents.ts` (sites
  ~160, 173, 231, 512, 703, 1015, 1107) and `captureServerAiGeneration` args.
- Prompt builders for the offline harness: `convex/agentPrompts.ts`
  (`buildSystemPrompt`, `buildUserPrompt`, `buildRubricReviewPrompt`, …).
- Bifrost provider wiring to mirror for the judge integration: `convex/agents.ts:77-90`.

## Verification
1. **Build**: `npx convex dev` (or `npx convex deploy --dry-run`) — confirm the OTel
   packages bundle in the Node action runtime (main risk; if esbuild chokes on the
   proto exporter, fall back to the OTLP **http/json** exporter or a hand-built span POST
   like `posthog.ts`).
2. **Trace lands**: trigger a persona-feedback action locally, then confirm the span in
   Arize (`ax trace`/UI) under project `twyne` with input/output + our metadata.
3. **Evaluator**: `ax evaluator ... trigger-run` on recent spans; confirm scores appear
   (never fabricate scores — report failures per skill rules).
4. **Experiment**: `npm run eval` → `ax experiments get` shows runs + eval columns.
5. **Dormant check**: unset `ARIZE_*` → actions still work, `flushArize()` no-ops.

## Out of scope (this pass)
Client-side tracing, annotation queues, prompt-optimization loop, compliance audit —
available via the other installed `arize-*` skills once A–C are in place.

content.text:
The plan is written. Here's the summary for approval.

## Plan: Persona reply responsiveness — live inline thread

**Problem:** Replying to an inline persona note generates the AI response correctly, but it only shows in the Cast side-panel — the popover closes and "in the text" nothing happens. The backend (`submitReply` → client `persona-reply` / Convex `runPersona`, both multi-turn-capable) already works; the gap is surfacing the thread back into the editor.

**Approach:** Extend the existing `twyne:*` window-event bus (no new shared state, no backend changes):
- **Panel** emits `twyne:persona-reply-thread`, `twyne:persona-replying`, and `twyne:persona-reply-error` from `submitReply`, and answers a `twyne:request-persona-thread` request.
- **Editor** popover gains `thread`/`replying`/`error` state, renders the conversation as bubbles with a typing indicator, **stays open** on send (instead of closing), and stays sticky mid-thread. Silent failures now surface inline.

**Touches 2 files:** `personas-panel.tsx` (emit/respond events) and `twyne-editor.tsx` (state + listeners + render thread + don't-close-on-reply). Reuses the shared `PersonaReply` type. Cast-panel sync stays intact since both read the same store/Convex writes.

Two follow-ups you raised remain queued after this: streaming responses, and the Twyne MCP server. Given your "use crush" preference for implementation, I can hand crush this plan once you approve.

# Persona reply responsiveness — live inline thread in the text

## Context

When the writer replies to an editor's **inline persona note** (the popover opened by
clicking a `.twyne-persona-note` span in the manuscript), the persona's AI answer is
generated correctly but only ever appears in the Cast **side-panel** thread — never back
in the text. The inline popover closes on send, so from the writer's point of view "in
the text" nothing happens. The user wants the model to respond **in the text**: the note
popover should become a live conversation (writer reply → typing indicator → persona
response → continue the back-and-forth) without leaving the editor.

The backend already supports this — `submitReply(noteId, askPersona)` in
`personas-panel.tsx` files the reply and calls client AI (`runClientAgent("persona-reply", …)`,
`src/utils/ai-client.ts:250`) or Convex `api.agents.runPersona` (`convex/agents.ts:597`,
`instruction:"elaborate"`), both of which accept `priorMessages` + `userMessage` for
multi-turn. The only gap is **surfacing the thread back into the editor popover**. The
editor and panel are separate components that already communicate over a `twyne:*` window
`CustomEvent` bus, so we extend that bus rather than add shared state.

## Approach

Keep `submitReply` as the single source of truth for filing replies + generating
responses. Add events so the panel streams thread state to the editor, and make the
editor popover render the thread + typing indicator and stay open.

### Event contract (new `twyne:*` events)
- Panel → editor `twyne:persona-reply-thread` `{ noteId, replies: PersonaReply[] }` — full
  current thread; emitted after the optimistic user-reply add and again after the persona
  response lands.
- Panel → editor `twyne:persona-replying` `{ noteId, replying: boolean }` — toggles the
  typing indicator around the AI call.
- Panel → editor `twyne:persona-reply-error` `{ noteId, message }` — surfaces the existing
  `store.conveneError` cases inline (today they fail silently from the popover's view).
- Editor → panel `twyne:request-persona-thread` `{ noteId }` — fired when a popover opens so
  prior history renders immediately.

Reuse the shared `PersonaReply` type from `src/types/index.ts:186` (already exported; used
via `src/utils/convex-sync.ts`).

### `src/components/personas/personas-panel.tsx`
- Add a small `$()` helper `emitReplyThread(noteId)` that dispatches
  `twyne:persona-reply-thread` with `store.repliesByNote[noteId] ?? []`.
- In `submitReply` (line ~763): after the optimistic user reply is pushed into
  `store.repliesByNote` (~786) call `emitReplyThread(noteId)`; when `askPersona`, dispatch
  `twyne:persona-replying {replying:true}` before the AI call and `{replying:false}` in the
  `finally` (~947); after the persona reply is appended (~927) call `emitReplyThread(noteId)`;
  on each existing error/early-return branch dispatch `twyne:persona-reply-error` with the
  same message currently assigned to `store.conveneError`.
- In the existing `useVisibleTask$` that listens for `twyne:persona-reply` (~958), add a
  `twyne:request-persona-thread` listener that calls `emitReplyThread(detail.noteId)`. The
  current `onReply` handler (`submitReply(detail.noteId, true)`) is unchanged.

### `src/components/editor/twyne-editor.tsx`
- Extend the `notePopover` store shape with `thread: PersonaReply[]`, `replying: boolean`,
  and `error: string | null` (import `PersonaReply` from `~/types`).
- Where the popover is populated from the clicked span's `data-persona-note-*` attributes
  (~577): initialize `thread: []`, `replying: false`, `error: null`, and dispatch
  `twyne:request-persona-thread {noteId: id}`.
- In the popover's existing `useVisibleTask$` listener block (~808, alongside
  `twyne:persona-notes`): add listeners for `twyne:persona-reply-thread`,
  `twyne:persona-replying`, `twyne:persona-reply-error` that update `store.notePopover` only
  when `detail.noteId === store.notePopover?.id`.
- Render the thread inside the popover body between the note text (~2363) and the reply
  textarea (~2377): map `thread` to chat bubbles — `authorKind:"user"` right-aligned in a
  neutral paper tone, `authorKind:"persona"` left-aligned tinted with `notePopover.color`;
  show a `typing…` bubble when `replying`; show `error` in a small warning line when set.
- On reply submit (the ⌘↩ handler ~2392 and the Reply button ~2429): **do not** set
  `store.notePopover = null`. Clear only the popover `draft` and keep the popover open.
  Dispatch `twyne:persona-reply` exactly as today; the thread updates via events. Keep
  "Strike" (dismiss) closing the popover.
- Keep the popover sticky during a conversation: when `replying` or `thread.length > 0`,
  treat it like `pinned` so the existing `onMouseLeave$` auto-close (~2303, ~660) does not
  dismiss it mid-thread.

### Route (`src/routes/editor/index.tsx`)
- No change required; it already reveals the Cast panel on `twyne:persona-reply`. Inline and
  Cast-panel threads stay in sync because both read the same `repliesByNote` / Convex
  `api.sync.addPersonaReply` writes `submitReply` already performs.

## Reuse
- `submitReply`, `runClientAgent("persona-reply")`, `api.agents.runPersona` — unchanged.
- Window `CustomEvent` bus pattern — same as `twyne:persona-reply` / `twyne:persona-notes`.
- `PersonaReply` type — `src/types/index.ts:186`.
- Existing popover styling tokens (`--color-paper*`, `notePopover.color`).

## Verification
1. `npm run dev`, open the editor, generate persona feedback so inline notes appear.
2. Click a `.twyne-persona-note`, type a reply, ⌘↩: popover stays open, your reply appears
   as a bubble, a `typing…` indicator shows, then the persona's response appears inline.
   Send a second reply → multi-turn continues in place.
3. Open the Cast panel → the same thread is mirrored (sync intact).
4. Failure surfacing: signed out with no provider configured, replying shows the inline
   error instead of doing nothing.

input.content:
Implement a feature in the Twyne repo (cwd = repo root). Edit ONLY these two files:
`src/components/personas/personas-panel.tsx` and `src/components/editor/twyne-editor.tsx`.
This is a Qwik app (useStore/$/useVisibleTask$, window CustomEvent bus). Keep the existing
style; tsc + prettier + eslint must stay clean. Do NOT change backend/Convex code.

GOAL — make persona replies happen "in the text". Today, replying to an inline persona note
(the `.twyne-persona-note` popover in the editor) generates the AI answer correctly but it
only shows in the Cast side-panel; the popover closes and the writer sees nothing inline.
Make the popover a LIVE CONVERSATION: the writer's reply, a "typing…" indicator, and the
persona's response appear inline, multi-turn, without leaving the editor.

ARCHITECTURE — reuse the existing `twyne:*` window CustomEvent bus (the editor popover and
the personas panel are separate components; they already talk via these events). Do NOT add
shared global state. Reuse the shared `PersonaReply` type exported from `src/types/index.ts`
(shape: { id, noteId, author, authorKind: "user"|"persona", personaId?, text, timestamp }).

NEW EVENTS:
- panel → editor  `twyne:persona-reply-thread`  detail `{ noteId: string, replies: PersonaReply[] }`
- panel → editor  `twyne:persona-replying`      detail `{ noteId: string, replying: boolean }`
- panel → editor  `twyne:persona-reply-error`   detail `{ noteId: string, message: string }`
- editor → panel  `twyne:request-persona-thread` detail `{ noteId: string }`

STUDY FIRST: read both files. In personas-panel.tsx the key function is `submitReply`
(around line 763) and the existing `useVisibleTask$` that adds a `twyne:persona-reply`
listener (around line 958). The reply thread state is `store.repliesByNote` (Record<noteId,
PersonaReply[]>), the in-flight flag is `store.isReplying`, and error strings are assigned to
`store.conveneError`. In twyne-editor.tsx the popover state is `store.notePopover` (populated
from the clicked span's `data-persona-note-*` attributes around line 577; it has fields like
id, author, color, label, note, quote, x, y, pinned, draft). The popover JSX is around lines
2289–2449; the note text renders ~2363 and the reply textarea ~2377; reply is dispatched via
`twyne:persona-reply` from the ⌘↩ handler (~2392) and the Reply button (~2429), both of which
currently set `store.notePopover = null` (the bug — they must NOT close anymore).

CHANGES — personas-panel.tsx:
1. Add a `$()` helper `emitReplyThread(noteId: string)` that dispatches
   `twyne:persona-reply-thread` with `{ noteId, replies: store.repliesByNote[noteId] ?? [] }`.
2. In `submitReply`:
   - Right after the optimistic user reply is added to `store.repliesByNote` (~786), call
     `emitReplyThread(noteId)`.
   - When `askPersona` is true: dispatch `twyne:persona-replying {noteId, replying:true}`
     before the AI work, and `{replying:false}` in the existing `finally` (~947).
   - After the persona reply is appended to `store.repliesByNote` (~927), call
     `emitReplyThread(noteId)`.
   - On EVERY early-return error branch that sets `store.conveneError` (the BYOK-failure, the
     no-response, and the runPersona-failure branches), also dispatch
     `twyne:persona-reply-error {noteId, message:<that same string>}` (and ensure replying is
     reset to false — the finally already covers the askPersona block; for branches that
     return before entering the try, dispatch replying:false too if you set it true).
3. In the existing `useVisibleTask$` that registers the `twyne:persona-reply` listener (~958),
   ALSO register a `twyne:request-persona-thread` listener that calls
   `emitReplyThread(detail.noteId)`. Clean both up. Leave the existing onReply handler
   (`submitReply(detail.noteId, true)`) unchanged.

CHANGES — twyne-editor.tsx:
1. Import `PersonaReply` from `~/types` (or the correct relative path used in this file for
   types). Extend the `notePopover` store type with `thread: PersonaReply[]`,
   `replying: boolean`, `error: string | null`.
2. Where `store.notePopover = { … }` is built from the span attributes (~577 area /
   wherever the object literal with id/author/color/note is assigned): add
   `thread: [], replying: false, error: null`, and immediately dispatch
   `window.dispatchEvent(new CustomEvent("twyne:request-persona-thread", { detail: { noteId: <the id> }}))`.
3. In the editor's existing `useVisibleTask$` listener block (~808, where
   `twyne:persona-notes` etc. are registered) add three listeners — `twyne:persona-reply-thread`,
   `twyne:persona-replying`, `twyne:persona-reply-error` — each updating `store.notePopover`
   ONLY when `store.notePopover && detail.noteId === store.notePopover.id`
   (set `.thread`, `.replying`, or `.error` respectively; reassign the object so Qwik tracks
   it). Clean all three up.
4. In the popover body, between the note text (~2363) and the reply textarea (~2377), render
   the conversation: map `store.notePopover.thread` to chat bubbles — `authorKind:"user"`
   right-aligned in a neutral paper tone (e.g. var(--color-paper-soft)/--color-paper-3),
   `authorKind:"persona"` left-aligned tinted with `store.notePopover.color`; each bubble
   shows author + text. When `store.notePopover.replying` is true, render a small "typing…"
   bubble (animated dots or italic "Mlle. … is typing…" using the author name). When
   `store.notePopover.error` is set, render a small warning line in a muted/danger tone.
   Match the existing popover typography tokens (font-serif/typewriter, --color-ink*).
5. In BOTH reply submit paths (⌘↩ handler ~2392 and Reply button ~2429): remove the
   `store.notePopover = null;` close. Instead keep the popover open and clear only the draft:
   `store.notePopover = { ...store.notePopover, draft: "", error: null };` then dispatch
   `twyne:persona-reply` as before. Keep the "Strike"/dismiss button closing the popover.
6. Prevent mid-conversation auto-close: in the popover `onMouseLeave$` (~2303) and any other
   auto-dismiss path that checks `pinned`, also bail out (do not close) when
   `store.notePopover.replying` or `store.notePopover.thread.length > 0`.

CONSTRAINTS:
- Two files only. No backend changes. Reuse `submitReply` as-is for the AI call.
- Keep event detail shapes EXACTLY as specified above (the two files must agree).
- Must pass: `npx tsc -p tsconfig.json --noEmit` (or the repo's `build.types`), prettier, and
  `eslint "src/**/*.ts*"`. Run prettier --write on the two files when done.
- Do NOT run `npm run dev` or anything needing a server/secrets. You may run typecheck/lint/
  prettier and `bun test src/components/editor` if quick.

   `twyne:persona-reply-thread` with `{ noteId, replies: store.repliesByNote[noteId] ?? [] }`.
2. In `submitReply`:
   - Right after the optimistic user reply is added to `store.repliesByNote` (~786), call
     `emitReplyThread(noteId)`.
   - When `askPersona` is true: dispatch `twyne:persona-replying {noteId, replying:true}`
     before the AI work, and `{replying:false}` in the existing `finally` (~947).
   - After the persona reply is appended to `store.repliesByNote` (~927), call
     `emitReplyThread(noteId)`.
   - On EVERY early-return error branch that sets `store.conveneError` (the BYOK-failure, the
     no-response, and the runPersona-failure branches), also dispatch
     `twyne:persona-reply-error {noteId, message:<that same string>}` (and ensure replying is
     reset to false — the finally already covers the askPersona block; for branches that
     return before entering the try, dispatch replying:false too if you set it true).
3. In the existing `useVisibleTask$` that registers the `twyne:persona-reply` listener (~958),
   ALSO register a `twyne:request-persona-thread` listener that calls
   `emitReplyThread(detail.noteId)`. Clean both up. Leave the existing onReply handler
   (`submitReply(detail.noteId, true)`) unchanged.

CHANGES — twyne-editor.tsx:
1. Import `PersonaReply` from `~/types` (or the correct relative path used in this file for
   types). Extend the `notePopover` store type with `thread: PersonaReply[]`,
   `replying: boolean`, `error: string | null`.
2. Where `store.notePopover = { … }` is built from the span attributes (~577 area /
   wherever the object literal with id/author/color/note is assigned): add
   `thread: [], replying: false, error: null`, and immediately dispatch
   `window.dispatchEvent(new CustomEvent("twyne:request-persona-thread", { detail: { noteId: <the id> }}))`.
3. In the editor's existing `useVisibleTask$` listener block (~808, where
   `twyne:persona-notes` etc. are registered) add three listeners — `twyne:persona-reply-thread`,
   `twyne:persona-replying`, `twyne:persona-reply-error` — each updating `store.notePopover`
   ONLY when `store.notePopover && detail.noteId === store.notePopover.id`
   (set `.thread`, `.replying`, or `.error` respectively; reassign the object so Qwik tracks
   it). Clean all three up.
4. In the popover body, between the note text (~2363) and the reply textarea (~2377), render
   the conversation: map `store.notePopover.thread` to chat bubbles — `authorKind:"user"`
   right-aligned in a neutral paper tone (e.g. var(--color-paper-soft)/--color-paper-3),
   `authorKind:"persona"` left-aligned tinted with `store.notePopover.color`; each bubble
   shows author + text. When `store.notePopover.replying` is true, render a small "typing…"
   bubble (animated dots or italic "Mlle. … is typing…" using the author name). When
   `store.notePopover.error` is set, render a small warning line in a muted/danger tone.
   Match the existing popover typography tokens (font-serif/typewriter, --color-ink*).
5. In BOTH reply submit paths (⌘↩ handler ~2392 and Reply button ~2429): remove the
   `store.notePopover = null;` close. Instead keep the popover open and clear only the draft:
   `store.notePopover = { ...store.notePopover, draft: "", error: null };` then dispatch
   `twyne:persona-reply` as before. Keep the "Strike"/dismiss button closing the popover.
6. Prevent mid-conversation auto-close: in the popover `onMouseLeave$` (~2303) and any other
   auto-dismiss path that checks `pinned`, also bail out (do not close) when
   `store.notePopover.replying` or `store.notePopover.thread.length > 0`.

CONSTRAINTS:
- Two files only. No backend changes. Reuse `submitReply` as-is for the AI call.
- Keep event detail shapes EXACTLY as specified above (the two files must agree).
- Must pass: `npx tsc -p tsconfig.json --noEmit` (or the repo's `build.types`), prettier, and
  `eslint "src/**/*.ts*"`. Run prettier --write on the two files when done.
- Do NOT run `npm run dev` or anything needing a server/secrets. You may run typecheck/lint/
  prettier and `bun test src/components/editor` if quick.
